805→### 6.4 Shared RAG Interface (All Surfaces)
   806→
   807→All three surfaces call the same RAG service via a unified gRPC interface:
   808→
   809→```protobuf
   810→service RavenRAG {
   811→  rpc Query(QueryRequest) returns (stream QueryResponse);
   812→  rpc CreateSession(CreateSessionRequest) returns (Session);
   813→  rpc GetSessionHistory(SessionHistoryRequest) returns (SessionHistory);
   814→}
   815→
   816→message QueryRequest {
   817→  string tenant_id = 1;
   818→  string session_id = 2;
   819→  string query_text = 3;    // typed message or STT transcription
   820→  map<string, string> metadata = 4;
   821→}
   822→
   823→message QueryResponse {
   824→  string token = 1;
   825→  repeated Source sources = 2;
   826→  bool is_final = 3;
   827→}
   828→```
   829→
   830→| Aspect | Chatbot | Voice Agent | WhatsApp |
   831→|--------|---------|-------------|----------|
   832→| Input | User-typed text | STT transcription | STT transcription |
   833→| Output | Streamed text (SSE) | Text -> TTS -> audio | Text -> TTS -> audio |
   834→| Session | Cookie/JWT + session ID | LiveKit Room metadata | WhatsApp call_id |
   835→| Latency tolerance | 1-3s acceptable | <1s target | <1s target |
   836→
   837→---
   838→
   839→## 7. Deployment
   840→
   841→### 7.1 Docker Compose Setup (Standard)
   842→
   843→| Service | Image | Exposed |
   844→|---------|-------|---------|
   845→| `go-api` | Custom build (~25 MB) | Yes (:8080) |
   846→| `python-worker` | Custom build | No |
   847→| `strapi` | Custom build | Yes (:1337) |
   848→| `keycloak` | `quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:26` | Yes (:8443) |
   849→| `postgres` | `pgvector/pgvector:pg18` + ParadeDB | No |
   850→| `valkey` | `valkey/valkey:8.1-alpine` | No |
   851→| `seaweedfs` | `chrislusf/seaweedfs` | No |
   852→| `traefik` | `traefik:v3.3` | Yes (:80/:443) |
   853→
   854→**Network:** All on `raven-internal` bridge. Only go-api, strapi, keycloak, traefik bind host ports.
   855→
   856→**Volumes:** `pg-data`, `kc-config` (realm exports, SPI JARs), `uploads` (SeaweedFS data), `valkey-data`.
   857→
   858→**Environment:** `.env` for non-secrets, `.env.secrets` (git-ignored) for credentials. `raven init` CLI scaffolds `.env.secrets` interactively.
   859→
   860→### 7.2 Edge Deployment (Raspberry Pi / ARM64)
   861→
   862→```yaml
   863→# docker-compose.edge.yml (simplified)
   864→services:
   865→  go-api:
   866→    image: raven/api:latest-arm64
   867→    platform: linux/arm64
   868→    ports: ["8080:8080"]
   869→    environment:
   870→      RAVEN_AI_WORKER_GRPC_ADDR: "cloud-server:50051"  # Remote Python worker
   871→      RAVEN_DB_HOST: postgres
   872→      RAVEN_VALKEY_HOST: valkey
   873→  postgres:
   874→    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg18
   875→    platform: linux/arm64
   876→    volumes: ["pg-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data"]
   877→  valkey:
   878→    image: valkey/valkey:8.1-alpine
   879→    platform: linux/arm64
   880→    command: ["valkey-server", "--maxmemory", "64mb"]
   881→```
   882→
   883→Go API binary: 10-25 MB, <50ms startup, 5-10 MB RAM. Cross-compiled: `GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 go build -ldflags="-s -w"`.
   884→

### 6.4 Shared RAG Interface (All Surfaces)

All three surfaces call the same RAG service via a unified gRPC interface:

```protobuf
service RavenRAG {
  rpc Query(QueryRequest) returns (stream QueryResponse);
  rpc CreateSession(CreateSessionRequest) returns (Session);
  rpc GetSessionHistory(SessionHistoryRequest) returns (SessionHistory);
}

message QueryRequest {
  string tenant_id = 1;
  string session_id = 2;
  string query_text = 3;    // typed message or STT transcription
  map<string, string> metadata = 4;
}

message QueryResponse {
  string token = 1;
  repeated Source sources = 2;
  bool is_final = 3;
}
```

| Aspect | Chatbot | Voice Agent | WhatsApp |
|--------|---------|-------------|----------|
| Input | User-typed text | STT transcription | STT transcription |
| Output | Streamed text (SSE) | Text -> TTS -> audio | Text -> TTS -> audio |
| Session | Cookie/JWT + session ID | LiveKit Room metadata | WhatsApp call_id |
| Latency tolerance | 1-3s acceptable | <1s target | <1s target |

---

## 7. Deployment

### 7.1 Docker Compose Setup (Standard)

| Service | Image | Exposed |
|---------|-------|---------|
| `go-api` | Custom build (~25 MB) | Yes (:8080) |
| `python-worker` | Custom build | No |
| `strapi` | Custom build | Yes (:1337) |
| `keycloak` | `quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:26` | Yes (:8443) |
| `postgres` | `pgvector/pgvector:pg18` + ParadeDB | No |
| `valkey` | `valkey/valkey:8.1-alpine` | No |
| `seaweedfs` | `chrislusf/seaweedfs` | No |
| `traefik` | `traefik:v3.3` | Yes (:80/:443) |

**Network:** All on `raven-internal` bridge. Only go-api, strapi, keycloak, traefik bind host ports.

**Volumes:** `pg-data`, `kc-config` (realm exports, SPI JARs), `uploads` (SeaweedFS data), `valkey-data`.

**Environment:** `.env` for non-secrets, `.env.secrets` (git-ignored) for credentials. `raven init` CLI scaffolds `.env.secrets` interactively.

### 7.2 Edge Deployment (Raspberry Pi / ARM64)

```yaml
# docker-compose.edge.yml (simplified)
services:
  go-api:
    image: raven/api:latest-arm64
    platform: linux/arm64
    ports: ["8080:8080"]
    environment:
      RAVEN_AI_WORKER_GRPC_ADDR: "cloud-server:50051"  # Remote Python worker
      RAVEN_DB_HOST: postgres
      RAVEN_VALKEY_HOST: valkey
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg18
    platform: linux/arm64
    volumes: ["pg-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data"]
  valkey:
    image: valkey/valkey:8.1-alpine
    platform: linux/arm64
    command: ["valkey-server", "--maxmemory", "64mb"]
```

Go API binary: 10-25 MB, <50ms startup, 5-10 MB RAM. Cross-compiled: `GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 go build -ldflags="-s -w"`.

input.prompt:
You are scaffolding the Go backend for the Raven platform. The repo is at the current working directory.

IMPORTANT: Use **Gin** (github.com/gin-gonic/gin) as the HTTP framework, NOT Echo.

## What to create

### 1. Go module
Initialize `github.com/ravencloak-org/Raven` with Go 1.23.

### 2. Project layout
```
cmd/
  api/
    main.go          -- entry point, Gin server startup
internal/
  config/
    config.go        -- viper-based configuration loading (server port, DB URL, Valkey URL, gRPC worker addr, OTEL endpoint)
  handler/
    health.go        -- GET /healthz handler
  middleware/        -- empty package (placeholder for JWT, RBAC, rate limiting later)
  model/            -- empty package (domain types)
  repository/       -- empty package (database access)
  service/          -- empty package (business logic)
  grpc/             -- empty package (gRPC client for Python AI worker)
pkg/
  apierror/
    apierror.go     -- shared error types: AppError struct, NewBadRequest, NewNotFound, NewUnauthorized, NewInternal helpers, Gin error handler middleware
  validator/        -- empty package (placeholder)
migrations/         -- empty directory (for goose SQL files later)
proto/              -- empty directory (protobuf definitions, will be created by Python worker agent)
```

For empty packages, create a minimal `.go` file with just the package declaration and a short doc comment.

### 3. Core dependencies (go get)
- github.com/gin-gonic/gin
- github.com/jackc/pgx/v5
- github.com/redis/go-redis/v9
- github.com/spf13/viper
- github.com/pressly/goose/v3
- google.golang.org/grpc
- google.golang.org/protobuf

### 4. cmd/api/main.go
- Load config via viper (with env var binding, prefix RAVEN_)
- Create Gin router (gin.Default())
- Register health check route: GET /healthz -> returns JSON {"status": "ok"}
- Graceful shutdown with signal handling (SIGINT, SIGTERM)
- Log startup message with port

### 5. internal/config/config.go
Use viper to load configuration with these fields:
```go
type Config struct {
    Server   ServerConfig
    Database DatabaseConfig
    Valkey   ValkeyConfig
    GRPC     GRPCConfig
}
type ServerConfig struct {
    Port int    // default 8080
    Mode string // default "debug"
}
type DatabaseConfig struct {
    URL string // RAVEN_DATABASE_URL
}
type ValkeyConfig struct {
    URL string // RAVEN_VALKEY_URL
}
type GRPCConfig struct {
    WorkerAddr string // RAVEN_GRPC_WORKER_ADDR, default "localhost:50051"
}
```
Bind env vars with RAVEN_ prefix. Support config file (config.yaml) but don't require it.

### 6. pkg/apierror/apierror.go
```go
type AppError struct {
    Code    int    `json:"code"`
    Message string `json:"message"`
    Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
}
```
With constructors: NewBadRequest, NewNotFound, NewUnauthorized, NewInternal.
Plus a Gin middleware/handler function that returns JSON error responses.

### 7. .air.toml
Configure air for hot-reload:
- Watch cmd/ and internal/ directories
- Build command: go build -o ./tmp/api ./cmd/api
- Run command: ./tmp/api
- Exclude: tmp/, vendor/, proto/

### 8. Makefile
Targets:
- `build`: go build -o bin/api ./cmd/api
- `run`: go run ./cmd/api
- `dev`: air (hot-reload)
- `test`: go test ./...
- `lint`: golangci-lint run
- `migrate-up`: goose -dir migrations postgres "$$DATABASE_URL" up
- `migrate-down`: goose -dir migrations postgres "$$DATABASE_URL" down
- `proto`: protoc generation (placeholder, just echo message for now)

### 9. .golangci.yml
Configure golangci-lint with sensible defaults:
- Enable: govet, errcheck, staticcheck, unused, gosimple, ineffassign, typecheck, revive
- Timeout: 5m

### 10. Verify
After creating everything:
- Run `go mod tidy`
- Run `go build ./cmd/api` to verify it compiles
- Commit all changes with a descriptive message

DO NOT create a README or any documentation files. Just the code.

input.prompt:
You are scaffolding the Vue.js frontend for the Raven platform. The repo is at the current working directory.

## What to create

### 1. Initialize with Vite
Create a `frontend/` directory and scaffold a Vue.js 3.5 + TypeScript project using Vite.

Run from the repo root:
```bash
cd frontend
npm create vite@latest . -- --template vue-ts
```

If that doesn't work (because directory exists), scaffold manually.

### 2. Project structure
```
frontend/
  src/
    assets/
      main.css          -- Tailwind CSS imports
    components/
      AppSidebar.vue    -- sidebar navigation component
      AppHeader.vue     -- top header with user menu placeholder
    composables/
      useAuth.ts        -- placeholder auth composable (getCurrentUser, isAuthenticated, logout)
    layouts/
      DefaultLayout.vue -- sidebar + header + router-view content area
      AuthLayout.vue    -- centered card layout for login/register
    pages/
      DashboardPage.vue -- "Welcome to Raven" placeholder
      LoginPage.vue     -- login form placeholder (will connect to Keycloak OIDC later)
      NotFoundPage.vue  -- 404 page
    router/
      index.ts          -- Vue Router setup with routes
    stores/
      auth.ts           -- Pinia auth store (placeholder: user state, login/logout actions)
    api/
      client.ts         -- Axios/fetch API client with base URL and auth interceptor placeholder
    types/
      index.ts          -- shared TypeScript types (User, Organization, Workspace, etc.)
    App.vue             -- root component with router-view
    main.ts             -- app entry point
  public/
    favicon.svg
  index.html
  vite.config.ts
  tsconfig.json
  tsconfig.app.json
  tsconfig.node.json
  package.json
  eslint.config.js
  .prettierrc
```

### 3. Dependencies to install
```bash
# Core
npm install vue-router@4 pinia @vueuse/core

# Tailwind CSS v4
npm install -D tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite

# Dev tools
npm install -D eslint @eslint/js typescript-eslint eslint-plugin-vue prettier eslint-config-prettier
```

NOTE: Tailwind CSS v4 does NOT use tailwind.config.ts or postcss.config.js. Instead:
- Add the @tailwindcss/vite plugin to vite.config.ts
- Import tailwindcss in the main CSS file: `@import "tailwindcss";`
- That's it. No config file needed for v4.

### 4. src/main.ts
```typescript
import { createApp } from 'vue'
import { createPinia } from 'pinia'
import router from './router'
import App from './App.vue'
import './assets/main.css'

const app = createApp(App)
app.use(createPinia())
app.use(router)
app.mount('#app')
```

### 5. src/assets/main.css
```css
@import "tailwindcss";
```

### 6. vite.config.ts
```typescript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import vue from '@vitejs/plugin-vue'
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [vue(), tailwindcss()],
  server: {
    port: 3000,
    proxy: {
      '/api': {
        target: 'http://localhost:8080',
        changeOrigin: true,
      },
    },
  },
})
```

### 7. Router (src/router/index.ts)
Routes:
- `/` -> redirect to /dashboard
- `/login` -> LoginPage (AuthLayout)
- `/dashboard` -> DashboardPage (DefaultLayout, requires auth - guard placeholder)
- `/:pathMatch(.*)*` -> NotFoundPage

### 8. Layouts
**DefaultLayout.vue**: Sidebar (fixed left, ~64px wide with icons) + header bar + main content area. Use Tailwind utility classes. Dark sidebar theme.

**AuthLayout.vue**: Centered card on a gradient background.

### 9. Pages
**DashboardPage.vue**: Simple welcome page with "Welcome to Raven" heading, a brief description placeholder, and some empty card placeholders for future widgets.

**LoginPage.vue**: Email + password form (non-functional, just the UI). "Sign in to Raven" heading. Note: actual auth will use Keycloak OIDC redirect, not this form - but it's a good placeholder.

**NotFoundPage.vue**: 404 with "Page not found" message and link back to dashboard.

### 10. Types (src/types/index.ts)
```typescript
export interface User {
  id: string
  email: string
  displayName: string
  orgId: string
  orgRole: 'owner' | 'admin' | 'member'
}

export interface Organization {
  id: string
  name: string
  slug: string
  status: 'active' | 'suspended'
}

export interface Workspace {
  id: string
  orgId: string
  name: string
  slug: string
}

export interface KnowledgeBase {
  id: string
  workspaceId: string
  name: string
  description: string
  documentCount: number
}
```

### 11. API client (src/api/client.ts)
Simple fetch wrapper with:
- Base URL from env (VITE_API_URL, default '/api')
- Auth header injection placeholder
- JSON request/response helpers
- Error handling

### 12. Auth store (src/stores/auth.ts)
Pinia store with:
- state: user (User | null), token (string | null), isAuthenticated (computed)
- actions: login (placeholder), logout, setUser
- This is a placeholder - real auth will use Keycloak OIDC

### 13. ESLint + Prettier config
eslint.config.js (flat config format):
- Vue plugin
- TypeScript plugin
- Prettier compatibility

.prettierrc:
- singleQuote: true
- semi: false
- trailingComma: 'all'
- printWidth: 100

### 14. Verify
After creating everything:
- Run `npm install` in frontend/
- Run `npm run build` to verify it compiles
- Run `npm run lint` to check for errors
- Commit all changes with a descriptive message

DO NOT create a README or documentation files. Just the code.
DO NOT use Tailwind Plus components (user has the subscription but we'd need their credentials to install it - just use standard Tailwind CSS v4 for now).

toolUseResult.prompt:
You are scaffolding the Python AI Worker for the Raven platform. The repo is at the current working directory.

## What to create

### 1. Proto definitions at the ROOT level: `proto/ai_worker.proto`
This is the shared protobuf definition used by both Go and Python. Create it at the repo root.

```protobuf
syntax = "proto3";

package raven.ai.v1;

option go_package = "github.com/ravencloak-org/Raven/internal/grpc/pb";

service AIWorker {
  rpc ParseAndEmbed (ParseRequest)      returns (ParseResponse);
  rpc QueryRAG      (RAGRequest)        returns (stream RAGChunk);
  rpc GetEmbedding  (EmbeddingRequest)  returns (EmbeddingResponse);
}

message ParseRequest {
  string document_id = 1;
  string org_id      = 2;
  string kb_id       = 3;
  bytes  content     = 4;
  string mime_type   = 5;
  string file_name   = 6;
}

message ParseResponse {
  string document_id   = 1;
  int32  chunk_count   = 2;
  string status        = 3;
  string error_message = 4;
}

message RAGRequest {
  string query            = 1;
  string org_id           = 2;
  repeated string kb_ids  = 3;
  string session_id       = 4;
  map<string, string> filters = 5;
  string model            = 6;
  string provider         = 7;
}

message RAGChunk {
  string text             = 1;
  bool   is_final         = 2;
  repeated Source sources = 3;
}

message Source {
  string document_id   = 1;
  string document_name = 2;
  string chunk_text    = 3;
  float  score         = 4;
}

message EmbeddingRequest {
  string text     = 1;
  string org_id   = 2;
  string model    = 3;
  string provider = 4;
}

message EmbeddingResponse {
  repeated float embedding = 1;
  int32 dimensions         = 2;
}
```

Also create `proto/buf.yaml` and `proto/buf.gen.yaml` for buf-based proto compilation (optional but nice to have).

### 2. Python project structure

```
ai-worker/
  raven_worker/
    __init__.py           -- version = "0.1.0"
    __main__.py           -- entry point: python -m raven_worker
    server.py             -- gRPC server setup, graceful shutdown
    config.py             -- settings via environment variables (pydantic-settings)
    services/
      __init__.py
      embedding.py        -- EmbeddingService stub (implements GetEmbedding RPC)
      rag.py              -- RAGService stub (implements QueryRAG streaming RPC)
    processors/
      __init__.py
      parser.py           -- LiteParse integration stub
      chunker.py          -- text chunking stub
      scraper.py          -- Crawl4AI integration stub
    providers/
      __init__.py
      base.py             -- EmbeddingProvider protocol/abstract base
      openai_provider.py  -- OpenAI embeddings stub
  proto/                  -- symlink to root proto/ OR just copy
  tests/
    __init__.py
    conftest.py           -- pytest fixtures
    test_server.py        -- basic server startup test
  pyproject.toml
  requirements.txt        -- pinned deps
  requirements-dev.txt    -- dev deps (ruff, pytest, grpcio-tools)
  Dockerfile
  Makefile
```

### 3. ai-worker/raven_worker/config.py
Use pydantic-settings:
```python
class Settings(BaseSettings):
    grpc_port: int = 50051
    grpc_max_workers: int = 10
    database_url: str = "postgresql://raven:raven@localhost:5432/raven"
    valkey_url: str = "redis://localhost:6379/0"
    otel_endpoint: str | None = None
    log_level: str = "INFO"

    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_prefix="RAVEN_")
```

### 4. ai-worker/raven_worker/server.py
- Create gRPC server with reflection enabled
- Register AIWorker servicer
- Add health check service (grpc_health)
- Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
- Structured logging setup

### 5. Service stubs
Each service method should have a proper stub that returns a reasonable default or raises grpc.StatusCode.UNIMPLEMENTED with a helpful message.

For QueryRAG (streaming): yield a single RAGChunk with text="Not implemented yet" and is_final=True.

### 6. ai-worker/raven_worker/providers/base.py
```python
from typing import Protocol

class EmbeddingProvider(Protocol):
    async def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]: ...
    
    @property
    def dimensions(self) -> int: ...
    
    @property  
    def model_name(self) -> str: ...
```

### 7. Generate Python gRPC stubs
Run the protoc/grpcio-tools compilation to generate `_pb2.py` and `_pb2_grpc.py` files from the proto.
Put generated files in `ai-worker/raven_worker/generated/`.

Create a script `ai-worker/scripts/generate_proto.sh` that does this generation.

### 8. ai-worker/pyproject.toml
```toml
[project]
name = "raven-worker"
version = "0.1.0"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "grpcio>=1.69.0",
    "grpcio-reflection>=1.69.0",
    "grpcio-health-checking>=1.69.0",
    "protobuf>=5.29.0",
    "pydantic-settings>=2.7.0",
    "structlog>=24.4.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "grpcio-tools>=1.69.0",
    "ruff>=0.9.0",
    "pytest>=8.3.0",
    "pytest-asyncio>=0.25.0",
]
```

### 9. ai-worker/requirements.txt
Pin the main dependencies.

### 10. ai-worker/Dockerfile
Multi-stage: builder installs deps, final stage is slim Python 3.12. 
Copy only necessary files. Run as non-root user.

### 11. ai-worker/Makefile
Targets:
- `proto`: generate proto stubs
- `run`: python -m raven_worker
- `test`: pytest
- `lint`: ruff check .
- `format`: ruff format .
- `install`: pip install -e ".[dev]"

### 12. Ruff config
Add ruff config in pyproject.toml:
- Line length: 100
- Target: py312
- Select: E, F, I, N, W, UP, B, A, SIM

### 13. Verify
- Run the proto generation script to produce the _pb2 files
- Run `ruff check ai-worker/` to verify no lint errors
- Commit all changes with a descriptive message

IMPORTANT: Use **Gin** (github.com/gin-gonic/gin) as the HTTP framework, NOT Echo.

## What to create

### 1. Go module
Initialize `github.com/ravencloak-org/Raven` with Go 1.23.

### 2. Project layout
```
cmd/
  api/
    main.go          -- entry point, Gin server startup
internal/
  config/
    config.go        -- viper-based configuration loading (server port, DB URL, Valkey URL, gRPC worker addr, OTEL endpoint)
  handler/
    health.go        -- GET /healthz handler
  middleware/        -- empty package (placeholder for JWT, RBAC, rate limiting later)
  model/            -- empty package (domain types)
  repository/       -- empty package (database access)
  service/          -- empty package (business logic)
  grpc/             -- empty package (gRPC client for Python AI worker)
pkg/
  apierror/
    apierror.go     -- shared error types: AppError struct, NewBadRequest, NewNotFound, NewUnauthorized, NewInternal helpers, Gin error handler middleware
  validator/        -- empty package (placeholder)
migrations/         -- empty directory (for goose SQL files later)
proto/              -- empty directory (protobuf definitions, will be created by Python worker agent)
```

For empty packages, create a minimal `.go` file with just the package declaration and a short doc comment.

### 3. Core dependencies (go get)
- github.com/gin-gonic/gin
- github.com/jackc/pgx/v5
- github.com/redis/go-redis/v9
- github.com/spf13/viper
- github.com/pressly/goose/v3
- google.golang.org/grpc
- google.golang.org/protobuf

### 4. cmd/api/main.go
- Load config via viper (with env var binding, prefix RAVEN_)
- Create Gin router (gin.Default())
- Register health check route: GET /healthz -> returns JSON {"status": "ok"}
- Graceful shutdown with signal handling (SIGINT, SIGTERM)
- Log startup message with port

### 5. internal/config/config.go
Use viper to load configuration with these fields:
```go
type Config struct {
    Server   ServerConfig
    Database DatabaseConfig
    Valkey   ValkeyConfig
    GRPC     GRPCConfig
}
type ServerConfig struct {
    Port int    // default 8080
    Mode string // default "debug"
}
type DatabaseConfig struct {
    URL string // RAVEN_DATABASE_URL
}
type ValkeyConfig struct {
    URL string // RAVEN_VALKEY_URL
}
type GRPCConfig struct {
    WorkerAddr string // RAVEN_GRPC_WORKER_ADDR, default "localhost:50051"
}
```
Bind env vars with RAVEN_ prefix. Support config file (config.yaml) but don't require it.

### 6. pkg/apierror/apierror.go
```go
type AppError struct {
    Code    int    `json:"code"`
    Message string `json:"message"`
    Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
}
```
With constructors: NewBadRequest, NewNotFound, NewUnauthorized, NewInternal.
Plus a Gin middleware/handler function that returns JSON error responses.

### 7. .air.toml
Configure air for hot-reload:
- Watch cmd/ and internal/ directories
- Build command: go build -o ./tmp/api ./cmd/api
- Run command: ./tmp/api
- Exclude: tmp/, vendor/, proto/

### 8. Makefile
Targets:
- `build`: go build -o bin/api ./cmd/api
- `run`: go run ./cmd/api
- `dev`: air (hot-reload)
- `test`: go test ./...
- `lint`: golangci-lint run
- `migrate-up`: goose -dir migrations postgres "$$DATABASE_URL" up
- `migrate-down`: goose -dir migrations postgres "$$DATABASE_URL" down
- `proto`: protoc generation (placeholder, just echo message for now)

### 9. .golangci.yml
Configure golangci-lint with sensible defaults:
- Enable: govet, errcheck, staticcheck, unused, gosimple, ineffassign, typecheck, revive
- Timeout: 5m

### 10. Verify
After creating everything:
- Run `go mod tidy`
- Run `go build ./cmd/api` to verify it compiles
- Commit all changes with a descriptive message

You are scaffolding the Vue.js frontend for the Raven platform. The repo is at the current working directory.

## What to create

### 1. Initialize with Vite
Create a `frontend/` directory and scaffold a Vue.js 3.5 + TypeScript project using Vite.

Run from the repo root:
```bash
cd frontend
npm create vite@latest . -- --template vue-ts
```

If that doesn't work (because directory exists), scaffold manually.

### 2. Project structure
```
frontend/
  src/
    assets/
      main.css          -- Tailwind CSS imports
    components/
      AppSidebar.vue    -- sidebar navigation component
      AppHeader.vue     -- top header with user menu placeholder
    composables/
      useAuth.ts        -- placeholder auth composable (getCurrentUser, isAuthenticated, logout)
    layouts/
      DefaultLayout.vue -- sidebar + header + router-view content area
      AuthLayout.vue    -- centered card layout for login/register
    pages/
      DashboardPage.vue -- "Welcome to Raven" placeholder
      LoginPage.vue     -- login form placeholder (will connect to Keycloak OIDC later)
      NotFoundPage.vue  -- 404 page
    router/
      index.ts          -- Vue Router setup with routes
    stores/
      auth.ts           -- Pinia auth store (placeholder: user state, login/logout actions)
    api/
      client.ts         -- Axios/fetch API client with base URL and auth interceptor placeholder
    types/
      index.ts          -- shared TypeScript types (User, Organization, Workspace, etc.)
    App.vue             -- root component with router-view
    main.ts             -- app entry point
  public/
    favicon.svg
  index.html
  vite.config.ts
  tsconfig.json
  tsconfig.app.json
  tsconfig.node.json
  package.json
  eslint.config.js
  .prettierrc
```

### 3. Dependencies to install
```bash
# Core
npm install vue-router@4 pinia @vueuse/core

# Tailwind CSS v4
npm install -D tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite

# Dev tools
npm install -D eslint @eslint/js typescript-eslint eslint-plugin-vue prettier eslint-config-prettier
```

NOTE: Tailwind CSS v4 does NOT use tailwind.config.ts or postcss.config.js. Instead:
- Add the @tailwindcss/vite plugin to vite.config.ts
- Import tailwindcss in the main CSS file: `@import "tailwindcss";`
- That's it. No config file needed for v4.

### 4. src/main.ts
```typescript
import { createApp } from 'vue'
import { createPinia } from 'pinia'
import router from './router'
import App from './App.vue'
import './assets/main.css'

const app = createApp(App)
app.use(createPinia())
app.use(router)
app.mount('#app')
```

### 5. src/assets/main.css
```css
@import "tailwindcss";
```

### 6. vite.config.ts
```typescript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import vue from '@vitejs/plugin-vue'
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [vue(), tailwindcss()],
  server: {
    port: 3000,
    proxy: {
      '/api': {
        target: 'http://localhost:8080',
        changeOrigin: true,
      },
    },
  },
})
```

### 7. Router (src/router/index.ts)
Routes:
- `/` -> redirect to /dashboard
- `/login` -> LoginPage (AuthLayout)
- `/dashboard` -> DashboardPage (DefaultLayout, requires auth - guard placeholder)
- `/:pathMatch(.*)*` -> NotFoundPage

### 8. Layouts
**DefaultLayout.vue**: Sidebar (fixed left, ~64px wide with icons) + header bar + main content area. Use Tailwind utility classes. Dark sidebar theme.

**AuthLayout.vue**: Centered card on a gradient background.

### 9. Pages
**DashboardPage.vue**: Simple welcome page with "Welcome to Raven" heading, a brief description placeholder, and some empty card placeholders for future widgets.

**LoginPage.vue**: Email + password form (non-functional, just the UI). "Sign in to Raven" heading. Note: actual auth will use Keycloak OIDC redirect, not this form - but it's a good placeholder.

**NotFoundPage.vue**: 404 with "Page not found" message and link back to dashboard.

### 10. Types (src/types/index.ts)
```typescript
export interface User {
  id: string
  email: string
  displayName: string
  orgId: string
  orgRole: 'owner' | 'admin' | 'member'
}

export interface Organization {
  id: string
  name: string
  slug: string
  status: 'active' | 'suspended'
}

export interface Workspace {
  id: string
  orgId: string
  name: string
  slug: string
}

export interface KnowledgeBase {
  id: string
  workspaceId: string
  name: string
  description: string
  documentCount: number
}
```

### 11. API client (src/api/client.ts)
Simple fetch wrapper with:
- Base URL from env (VITE_API_URL, default '/api')
- Auth header injection placeholder
- JSON request/response helpers
- Error handling

### 12. Auth store (src/stores/auth.ts)
Pinia store with:
- state: user (User | null), token (string | null), isAuthenticated (computed)
- actions: login (placeholder), logout, setUser
- This is a placeholder - real auth will use Keycloak OIDC

### 13. ESLint + Prettier config
eslint.config.js (flat config format):
- Vue plugin
- TypeScript plugin
- Prettier compatibility

.prettierrc:
- singleQuote: true
- semi: false
- trailingComma: 'all'
- printWidth: 100

### 14. Verify
After creating everything:
- Run `npm install` in frontend/
- Run `npm run build` to verify it compiles
- Run `npm run lint` to check for errors
- Commit all changes with a descriptive message

DO NOT create a README or documentation files. Just the code.
DO NOT use Tailwind Plus components (user has the subscription but we'd need their credentials to install it - just use standard Tailwind CSS v4 for now).

   133→
   134→### License Risk Notes
   135→
   136→**ParadeDB (AGPL-3.0):** Using ParadeDB in a SaaS product triggers AGPL copyleft obligations. Mitigation options:
   137→1. Purchase a ParadeDB commercial license (recommended for production)
   138→2. Fall back to PostgreSQL native `tsvector` + `ts_rank` for BM25-equivalent full-text search (no ParadeDB dependency)
   139→3. Use ParadeDB in development/staging, PostgreSQL native tsvector in production until commercial license is obtained
   140→
   141→The codebase MUST abstract the full-text search layer behind an interface so the ParadeDB implementation can be swapped for native tsvector without code changes.
   142→
   143→**Redis replaced by Valkey:** Redis adopted tri-licensing (RSALv2/SSPLv1/AGPLv3) since v8.0. Valkey (BSD-3-Clause, Linux Foundation fork of Redis 7.2) is a drop-in API-compatible replacement with no licensing risk.
   144→
   145→**MinIO replaced by SeaweedFS:** MinIO is AGPL-3.0. SeaweedFS (Apache 2.0) provides S3-compatible object storage. For simple deployments, local filesystem storage is also supported.
   146→
   147→**Firecrawl replaced by Crawl4AI:** Firecrawl is AGPL-3.0. Crawl4AI (Apache 2.0) is Python-native, integrates directly into the Python AI worker, and has built-in chunking strategies.
   148→
   149→**Coqui TTS replaced by Piper TTS:** Coqui XTTS model weights are non-commercial only (CPML) and the company is defunct. Piper TTS (MIT, archived rhasspy/piper version) is the license-safe replacement. The active fork (OHF-Voice/piper1-gpl) is GPL-3.0 which is acceptable for server-side SaaS use (not triggered by network access, only distribution).
   150→
   151→**OpenObserve (AGPL-3.0):** Safe when used as an unmodified infrastructure service. Raven communicates with OpenObserve via standard OTLP/HTTP endpoints; AGPL copyleft applies only to OpenObserve itself, not to Raven's code. Any modifications to OpenObserve's source must be released under AGPL. Enterprise edition available for strict no-AGPL policies.
   152→
   153→**k6 (AGPL-3.0):** k6 is a CLI load-testing tool run by developers and CI pipelines. It is never deployed as part of Raven's production stack. AGPL does not apply to tools used internally.
   154→
   155→**ClamAV (GPL-2.0):** Runs as a separate daemon process (`clamd`). Raven communicates via TCP socket. GPL-2.0 is server-side safe for SaaS (no distribution to end users).
   156→
   157→**TEN Framework REJECTED:** License includes anti-competition clauses and restrictions on enabling third parties. Agora RTC is the only WebRTC transport (proprietary, paid). LiveKit (Apache 2.0, fully self-hostable) is used instead.
   158→
   159→---
   160→
   161→## 3. System Architecture
   162→
   163→### 3.1 High-Level Component Diagram
   164→
   165→```
   166→+-------------------------------------------------------------------------+
   167→|                            CLIENTS                                      |
   168→|       Vue.js + Tailwind Plus (SPA)  |  <raven-chat> Web Component      |
   169→+----------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
   170→                             | HTTPS
   171→                             v
   172→+--------------------------------------------------------------------------+
   173→|                        REVERSE PROXY (Traefik)                           |
   174→|    /api/* --> Go API   /cms/* --> Strapi   /auth/* --> Keycloak           |
   175→+----+------------------+--------------------+------------------+----------+
   176→     |                  |                    |                  |
   177→     v                  v                    v                  v
   178→+---------+    +--------------+    +--------------+   +--------------+
   179→| Keycloak|    |  Go API      |    |   Strapi     |   |   Valkey     |
   180→| + reaven|<---|  Server      |--->|   CMS        |   |  (Job Queue) |
   181→|   cloak |    |  (Echo)      |    |              |   |              |
   182→|   SPI   |    +--+-+-+-------+    +------+-------+   +------+-------+
   183→+---------+       | | |                   |                  |
   184→                  | | |  gRPC             |                  |
   185→                  | | v                   |                  |
   186→                  | | +-------------------+------------------+---+
   187→                  | | |         Python AI Worker                 |
   188→                  | | |  +----------+  +-------------------+    |
   189→                  | | |  | Embedding|  |   RAG Query       |    |
   190→                  | | |  | Pipeline |  |   Engine          |    |
   191→                  | | |  +----+-----+  +-------------------+    |
   192→                  | | |       | subprocess/CLI                  |
   193→                  | | |  +----v-----------------+               |
   194→                  | | |  | LiteParse (Node.js)  |               |
   195→                  | | |  | Document Parsing     |               |
   196→                  | | |  +----------------------+               |
   197→                  | | +------------------------------------------+
   198→                  | |             |
   199→                  v v             v
   200→              +-----------------------------+     +------------------+
   201→              |       PostgreSQL 18         |     |    SeaweedFS     |
   202→              |  + pgvector (embeddings)    |     |  (Object Store)  |
   203→              |  + ParadeDB (full-text/BM25)|     +------------------+
   204→              +-----------------------------+
   205→```
   206→
   207→### 3.2 Service Breakdown
   208→
   209→| Service | Role | Exposed |
   210→|---------|------|---------|
   211→| **Go API** | Primary API gateway. JWT validation, routing, tenant resolution, orchestration. REST API for CRUD, enqueues async jobs, delegates AI to Python via gRPC. | Yes (:8080) |
   212→| **Python AI Worker** | All AI/ML workloads. gRPC server for RAG queries, embedding generation. Consumes Valkey jobs for async document processing. Runs Crawl4AI for web scraping. | No (internal gRPC) |
   213→| **LiteParse** | Document-to-text extraction (PDF, DOCX, images/OCR). Invoked by Python worker as subprocess. | No (co-located) |
   214→| **Strapi** | Headless CMS for platform marketing content (landing pages, docs, help articles) and as a quick admin UI for seed data during early development. Not in the critical path. Can be dropped if Vue.js admin dashboard covers all needs. | Yes (:1337) |
   215→| **Keycloak** | Identity provider. OIDC/OAuth2, user management, reavencloak SPI for custom claims. | Yes (:8443) |
   216→| **PostgreSQL 18** | Primary datastore. pgvector for embeddings, ParadeDB for BM25 full-text (or native tsvector as fallback). RLS for tenant isolation. | No (internal) |
   217→| **Valkey** | Job queue for async processing, rate limiting, caching. Drop-in Redis replacement, BSD-3-Clause. | No (internal) |
   218→| **SeaweedFS** | S3-compatible object storage for uploaded files. Apache 2.0, replaces MinIO. | No (internal) |
   219→
   220→### 3.3 Edge Deployment Mode (Split Architecture)
   221→
   222→For Raspberry Pi and edge devices, Raven runs in a split configuration:
   223→
   224→```
   225→+---------------------------+          +---------------------------+
   226→|   EDGE DEVICE (Pi 5)     |          |   REMOTE SERVER (Cloud)   |
   227→|                           |   gRPC   |                           |
   228→|  Go API Server (Echo)     |<-------->|  Python AI Worker         |
   229→|  - JWT validation         |  over    |  - Embedding generation   |
   230→|  - REST API serving       |  network |  - RAG query engine       |
   231→|  - Valkey (embedded/tiny) |          |  - LiteParse subprocess   |
   232→|  - PostgreSQL 18 (local)  |          |  - Crawl4AI scraping      |
   233→|    + pgvector             |          |  - LLM API calls (BYOK)  |
   234→|    + tsvector (no ParadeDB|          |                           |
   235→|      on ARM64)            |          |  LiveKit Server (if voice)|
   236→|                           |          |  SeaweedFS (object store) |
   237→|  Docker image: ~25 MB     |          |                           |
   238→|  RAM: 5-10 MB idle        |          +---------------------------+
   239→|  Startup: <50ms           |
   240→+---------------------------+
   241→```
   242→
   243→**Key design constraints for edge mode:**
   244→- Go API binary cross-compiled for ARM64: `GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 go build`
   245→- PostgreSQL runs locally on edge; pgvector is ARM64-compatible; ParadeDB may not be (fall back to native tsvector)
   246→- Python AI worker runs on a remote server with GPU access
   247→- gRPC connection between edge and cloud over TLS with mutual authentication
   248→- Valkey runs locally on edge in minimal-memory mode
   249→- SeaweedFS or local filesystem for file storage on edge
   250→

   254→service AIWorker {

### License Risk Notes

**ParadeDB (AGPL-3.0):** Using ParadeDB in a SaaS product triggers AGPL copyleft obligations. Mitigation options:

**MinIO replaced by SeaweedFS:** MinIO is AGPL-3.0. SeaweedFS (Apache 2.0) provides S3-compatible object storage. For simple deployments, local filesystem storage is also supported.

**Firecrawl replaced by Crawl4AI:** Firecrawl is AGPL-3.0. Crawl4AI (Apache 2.0) is Python-native, integrates directly into the Python AI worker, and has built-in chunking strategies.

**Coqui TTS replaced by Piper TTS:** Coqui XTTS model weights are non-commercial only (CPML) and the company is defunct. The active fork (OHF-Voice/piper1-gpl) is GPL-3.0 which is acceptable for server-side SaaS use (not triggered by network access, only distribution).

**OpenObserve (AGPL-3.0):** Safe when used as an unmodified infrastructure service. Raven communicates with OpenObserve via standard OTLP/HTTP endpoints; AGPL copyleft applies only to OpenObserve itself, not to Raven's code. Any modifications to OpenObserve's source must be released under AGPL. Enterprise edition available for strict no-AGPL policies.

**k6 (AGPL-3.0):** k6 is a CLI load-testing tool run by developers and CI pipelines. It is never deployed as part of Raven's production stack. AGPL does not apply to tools used internally.

**ClamAV (GPL-2.0):** Runs as a separate daemon process (`clamd`). Raven communicates via TCP socket. GPL-2.0 is server-side safe for SaaS (no distribution to end users).

**TEN Framework REJECTED:** License includes anti-competition clauses and restrictions on enabling third parties. Agora RTC is the only WebRTC transport (proprietary, paid). LiveKit (Apache 2.0, fully self-hostable) is used instead.

---

## 3. System Architecture

### 3.1 High-Level Component Diagram

```
+-------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                            CLIENTS                                      |
|       Vue.js + Tailwind Plus (SPA)  |  <raven-chat> Web Component      |
+----------------------------+--------------------------------------------+
                             | HTTPS
                             v
+--------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                        REVERSE PROXY (Traefik)                           |
|    /api/* --> Go API   /cms/* --> Strapi   /auth/* --> Keycloak           |
+----+------------------+--------------------+------------------+----------+
     |                  |                    |                  |
     v                  v                    v                  v
+---------+    +--------------+    +--------------+   +--------------+
| Keycloak|    |  Go API      |    |   Strapi     |   |   Valkey     |
| + reaven|<---|  Server      |--->|   CMS        |   |  (Job Queue) |
|   cloak |    |  (Echo)      |    |              |   |              |
|   SPI   |    +--+-+-+-------+    +------+-------+   +------+-------+
+---------+       | | |                   |                  |
                  | | |  gRPC             |                  |
                  | | v                   |                  |
                  | | +-------------------+------------------+---+
                  | | |         Python AI Worker                 |
                  | | |  +----------+  +-------------------+    |
                  | | |  | Embedding|  |   RAG Query       |    |
                  | | |  | Pipeline |  |   Engine          |    |
                  | | |  +----+-----+  +-------------------+    |
                  | | |       | subprocess/CLI                  |
                  | | |  +----v-----------------+               |
                  | | |  | LiteParse (Node.js)  |               |
                  | | |  | Document Parsing     |               |
                  | | |  +----------------------+               |
                  | | +------------------------------------------+
                  | |             |
                  v v             v
              +-----------------------------+     +------------------+
              |       PostgreSQL 18         |     |    SeaweedFS     |
              |  + pgvector (embeddings)    |     |  (Object Store)  |
              |  + ParadeDB (full-text/BM25)|     +------------------+
              +-----------------------------+
```

### 3.2 Service Breakdown

| Service | Role | Exposed |
|---------|------|---------|
| **Go API** | Primary API gateway. JWT validation, routing, tenant resolution, orchestration. REST API for CRUD, enqueues async jobs, delegates AI to Python via gRPC. | Yes (:8080) |
| **Python AI Worker** | All AI/ML workloads. gRPC server for RAG queries, embedding generation. Consumes Valkey jobs for async document processing. Runs Crawl4AI for web scraping. | No (internal gRPC) |
| **LiteParse** | Document-to-text extraction (PDF, DOCX, images/OCR). Invoked by Python worker as subprocess. | No (co-located) |
| **Strapi** | Headless CMS for platform marketing content (landing pages, docs, help articles) and as a quick admin UI for seed data during early development. Not in the critical path. Can be dropped if Vue.js admin dashboard covers all needs. | Yes (:1337) |
| **Keycloak** | Identity provider. OIDC/OAuth2, user management, reavencloak SPI for custom claims. | Yes (:8443) |
| **PostgreSQL 18** | Primary datastore. pgvector for embeddings, ParadeDB for BM25 full-text (or native tsvector as fallback). RLS for tenant isolation. | No (internal) |
| **Valkey** | Job queue for async processing, rate limiting, caching. Drop-in Redis replacement, BSD-3-Clause. | No (internal) |
| **SeaweedFS** | S3-compatible object storage for uploaded files. Apache 2.0, replaces MinIO. | No (internal) |

### 3.3 Edge Deployment Mode (Split Architecture)

For Raspberry Pi and edge devices, Raven runs in a split configuration:

```
+---------------------------+          +---------------------------+
|   EDGE DEVICE (Pi 5)     |          |   REMOTE SERVER (Cloud)   |
|                           |   gRPC   |                           |
|  Go API Server (Echo)     |<-------->|  Python AI Worker         |
|  - JWT validation         |  over    |  - Embedding generation   |
|  - REST API serving       |  network |  - RAG query engine       |
|  - Valkey (embedded/tiny) |          |  - LiteParse subprocess   |
|  - PostgreSQL 18 (local)  |          |  - Crawl4AI scraping      |
|    + pgvector             |          |  - LLM API calls (BYOK)  |
|    + tsvector (no ParadeDB|          |                           |
|      on ARM64)            |          |  LiveKit Server (if voice)|
|                           |          |  SeaweedFS (object store) |
|  Docker image: ~25 MB     |          |                           |
|  RAM: 5-10 MB idle        |          +---------------------------+
|  Startup: <50ms           |
+---------------------------+
```

**Key design constraints for edge mode:**
- Go API binary cross-compiled for ARM64: `GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 go build`
- PostgreSQL runs locally on edge; pgvector is ARM64-compatible; ParadeDB may not be (fall back to native tsvector)
- Python AI worker runs on a remote server with GPU access
- gRPC connection between edge and cloud over TLS with mutual authentication
- Valkey runs locally on edge in minimal-memory mode
- SeaweedFS or local filesystem for file storage on edge

### 3.4 Go <-> Python gRPC Interface

```protobuf
service AIWorker {

**Upload Document:**
```
Client --> Go API POST /api/v1/orgs/{org}/documents [multipart]
  Go API: validate JWT + org membership
  Go API: store file to SeaweedFS
  Go API: INSERT document (status: "queued") into PostgreSQL
  Go API: ENQUEUE job into Valkey
       <-- 202 { doc_id, status: "queued" }

--- Async (Python Worker) ---
  Worker: DEQUEUE job
  Worker: invoke LiteParse CLI --> JSON text output
  Worker: chunk text (512 tokens, 50 overlap)
  Worker: embed chunks via org's configured provider (BYOK)
  Worker: INSERT chunks + embeddings into PostgreSQL
  Worker: UPDATE document status --> "ready"
```

**Chat / RAG Query:**
```
Client --> Go API POST /api/v1/orgs/{org}/chat { query, conversation_id }
  Go API: validate JWT
  Go API: gRPC server-streaming call to Python Worker

  Python Worker:
    1. Embed query --> query_vector
    2. Hybrid search: pgvector (cosine) + ParadeDB (BM25) or tsvector
    3. RRF fusion to merge results
    4. Rerank top-K
    5. Stream LLM completion (org's BYOK provider)

  Go API: forward tokens as SSE events --> Client
```

### 3.6 Job Queue Design (Valkey)

| Queue | Consumer | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|
| `raven:jobs:document_process` | Python Worker | Parse, chunk, embed, index |
| `raven:jobs:document_process:failed` | -- | Dead-letter queue |
| `raven:jobs:web_scrape` | Python Worker | Crawl4AI web scraping |
| `raven:jobs:reindex` | Python Worker | Re-embed on model change |

Job lifecycle: `pending --> processing --> done` (or `failed` after 3 retries with exponential backoff). Visibility timeout: 300s. Max TTL: 30 minutes.

   558→### 4.3 Multi-Tenancy via RLS
   559→
   560→Shared schema with Row-Level Security. All tenants share one database.
   561→
   562→```sql
   563→ALTER TABLE workspaces ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
   564→
   565→CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON workspaces
   566→    FOR ALL
   567→    USING (org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id')::uuid)
   568→    WITH CHECK (org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id')::uuid);
   569→
   570→-- Admin bypass for cross-tenant operations
   571→CREATE POLICY admin_bypass ON workspaces
   572→    FOR ALL TO raven_admin
   573→    USING (true);
   574→```
   575→
   576→Go API middleware sets `SET app.current_org_id = '<uuid>'` on every request from JWT claims.
   577→
   578→**Tables with RLS:** `workspaces`, `users`, `workspace_members`, `knowledge_bases`, `documents`, `sources`, `chunks`, `embeddings`, `llm_provider_configs`, `chat_sessions`, `chat_messages`, `voice_sessions`, `voice_turns`, `api_keys`.
   579→
   580→### 4.4 Document Processing State Machine
   581→
   582→```
   583→queued --> crawling* --> parsing --> chunking --> embedding --> ready
   584→  |           |            |           |             |
   585→  +-----------+------------+-----------+-------------+--> failed
   586→                                                          |
   587→                                                          v
   588→                                                     reprocessing --> parsing
   589→```
   590→*`crawling` only for Sources (web scraper fetches URLs first).
   591→
   592→| State | Description | Valid transitions |
   593→|-------|-------------|-------------------|
   594→| `queued` | Created, waiting in processing queue | `parsing`, `crawling` (sources only) |
   595→| `crawling` | Web scraper fetching URL content | `parsing`, `failed` |
   596→| `parsing` | Extracting text from file | `chunking`, `failed` |
   597→| `chunking` | Splitting extracted text into chunks | `embedding`, `failed` |
   598→| `embedding` | Generating vector embeddings | `ready`, `failed` |
   599→| `ready` | Complete -- available for retrieval | `reprocessing` |
   600→| `failed` | Error occurred | `reprocessing` |

   602→
   603→### 4.5 Access Control

   607→| **org_admin** | Organization | Full control over all workspaces, billing, org settings |

file.content:
| `api_key_iv` | BYTEA | Initialization vector |
| `api_key_hint` | VARCHAR(20) | Last 4 chars for UI |
| `base_url` | TEXT | Override for custom endpoints |
| `config` | JSONB | Provider-specific settings |
| `is_default` | BOOLEAN | |
| `status` | ENUM | `active`, `revoked`, `expired` |
| `created_by` | UUID FK -> users | |
| `created_at` / `updated_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ | |

Encryption: AES-256-GCM with master key in secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, or env var fallback). Per-org data encryption keys (DEKs). Keys never logged or returned in API responses.

**API Keys** -- for embeddable chatbot authentication

| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `id` | UUID PK | |
| `org_id` | UUID FK | |
| `knowledge_base_id` | UUID FK | |
| `key_hash` | VARCHAR(128) | SHA-256 hash of the key |
| `key_prefix` | VARCHAR(20) | `rk_live_...` prefix for identification |
| `name` | VARCHAR(255) | User-given label |
| `allowed_domains` | TEXT[] | Domain allow-list for CORS |
| `rate_limit` | INTEGER | Requests per minute |
| `status` | ENUM | `active`, `revoked` |
| `created_by` | UUID FK -> users | |
| `created_at` / `expires_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ | |

**Chat Sessions / Messages** -- high-throughput

| Column (Session) | Type | Notes |
|-------------------|------|-------|
| `id` | UUID PK | |
| `org_id` | UUID FK | |
| `knowledge_base_id` | UUID FK | |
| `user_id` | UUID FK (nullable) | Nullable for anonymous chatbot users |
| `session_token` | VARCHAR(255) | Client-generated for anonymous |
| `metadata` | JSONB | Channel, user-agent, etc. |
| `created_at` / `expires_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ | 24h TTL for anonymous |

| Column (Message) | Type | Notes |
|-------------------|------|-------|
| `id` | UUID PK | |
| `session_id` | UUID FK | |
| `org_id` | UUID FK | |
| `role` | ENUM | `user`, `assistant`, `system` |
| `content` | TEXT | |
| `token_count` | INTEGER | |
| `chunk_ids` | UUID[] | Retrieved chunks for this response |
| `model_name` | VARCHAR(100) | LLM model used |
| `latency_ms` | INTEGER | Response generation time |
| `created_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ | |

**Voice Sessions / Turns** -- structured similarly to chat, with additional fields for audio duration, STT/TTS latency, and LiveKit room ID.

**Processing Events** -- audit log for document processing

| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `id` | UUID PK | |
| `org_id` | UUID FK | |
| `document_id` | UUID FK (nullable) | |
| `source_id` | UUID FK (nullable) | |
| `from_status` | VARCHAR(20) | |
| `to_status` | VARCHAR(20) | |
| `error_message` | TEXT | |
| `duration_ms` | INTEGER | Time in this state |
| `created_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ | |

### 4.3 Multi-Tenancy via RLS

Shared schema with Row-Level Security. All tenants share one database.

```sql
ALTER TABLE workspaces ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON workspaces
    FOR ALL
    USING (org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id')::uuid)
    WITH CHECK (org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id')::uuid);

-- Admin bypass for cross-tenant operations
CREATE POLICY admin_bypass ON workspaces
    FOR ALL TO raven_admin
    USING (true);
```

Go API middleware sets `SET app.current_org_id = '<uuid>'` on every request from JWT claims.

**Tables with RLS:** `workspaces`, `users`, `workspace_members`, `knowledge_bases`, `documents`, `sources`, `chunks`, `embeddings`, `llm_provider_configs`, `chat_sessions`, `chat_messages`, `voice_sessions`, `voice_turns`, `api_keys`.

### 4.4 Document Processing State Machine

```
queued --> crawling* --> parsing --> chunking --> embedding --> ready
  |           |            |           |             |
  +-----------+------------+-----------+-------------+--> failed
                                                          |
                                                          v
                                                     reprocessing --> parsing
```
*`crawling` only for Sources (web scraper fetches URLs first).

| State | Description | Valid transitions |
|-------|-------------|-------------------|
| `queued` | Created, waiting in processing queue | `parsing`, `crawling` (sources only) |
| `crawling` | Web scraper fetching URL content | `parsing`, `failed` |
| `parsing` | Extracting text from file | `chunking`, `failed` |
| `chunking` | Splitting extracted text into chunks | `embedding`, `failed` |
| `embedding` | Generating vector embeddings | `ready`, `failed` |
| `ready` | Complete -- available for retrieval | `reprocessing` |
| `failed` | Error occurred | `reprocessing` |
| `reprocessing` | Manual retry; clears old chunks | `parsing`, `crawling` |

### 4.5 Access Control

| Role | Scope | Permissions |
|------|-------|-------------|
| **org_admin** | Organization | Full control over all workspaces, billing, org settings |
| **owner** | Workspace | Full control, delete workspace, transfer ownership |
| **admin** | Workspace | Manage KBs, documents, members (except owners) |
